name: Prime Value Score (PVS)
description: Scores a player on their best sustained multi-year prime, independent of which specific award they are tagged with in players_data.json. The 'category' field in players_data.json is metadata for filtering/grouping only and does not affect baseValue.

peakImpact.definition: Average per-possession statistical impact (BPM/EPM-equivalent) across the player's best 3-5 consecutive seasons.

accoladeDensity.definition: Rate of All-NBA/All-Defense/All-Star selections earned during the prime window, weighted by tier (1st team=3, 2nd=2, 3rd=1, All-Star=1), averaged per year in the window.

teamSuccess.definition: Blend of average team win% and deepest playoff round reached during the prime window.

primeLongevity.definition: How many consecutive years the elite level was sustained, scaled/capped around a 6-year ceiling.

notes: category in players_data.json is for UI grouping/filtering only — it carries zero weight in baseValue.
primeWindow is the multi-year stretch representing a player's peak sustained level, not necessarily the season they won their specific award.
Active/young players (e.g., Wembanyama, Mobley, Edwards) have provisional scores since their prime is still unfolding.
teamSuccess in players_data.json is currently solved algebraically (so the weighted sum reproduces baseValue) as a placeholder — swap in real win%/playoff data once available and drop the solver.
pseudocode:
def calculate_pvs(peak_impact, accolade_density, team_success, prime_longevity):
    return (0.40*peak_impact + 0.25*accolade_density + 0.20*team_success + 0.15*prime_longevity)
